detail: Cycle 3 (sequence): macro-iteration Outcome C from ple_fraud_txn_amount. Reformulated hypothesis: sequence-model amount signal is amount-IN-CONTEXT (deviation vs card history); deviation feature helps, PLE-of-raw adds little. GRU PoC on synthetic per-card sequences; real IEEE-CIS sequences at Step 6. Carrying conclusions_only + debate from cycle 2.

Sequence GRU PoC: seq_raw 0.964 >> tab_last_raw 0.425 (+0.539 = sequence context is the signal). seq_dev-seq_raw -0.003 (explicit deviation adds nothing - GRU learns context from raw). seq_ple-seq_raw -0.014 (PLE adds nothing). Answers user: sequence model captures amount-in-context from RAW amount; encoding it doesn't help.
Step 2 README: sequence hypothesis, GRU pipeline, PoC output, scope exclusions (synthetic-clean caveat, no CIs, no shuffled-history lever, strawman baseline)

Step 2 intent review: causal deviation verified (running stats through t-1, no leakage); PLE/scaler train-fit. Key flag: synthetic signal cleanly learnable -> no headroom for deviation/PLE (real-data test needed). No PoC bug.
Stage A.1: ml-critic R1 on sequence PoC; JSON-first

Step 6 data: chose account-sequence dataset at ~/Dropbox/GitHub/demo/tmp/data over IEEE-CIS (no clean UID). Clean accountNumber key, transactionDateTime, transactionAmount, isFraud; ~7% fraud; temporal pre-split (train Jan-Aug/valid Sep-Oct/test Nov-Dec), accounts span splits. Build causal per-account sequences. Exclude *FraudTrend leakage features. Updated HYPOTHESIS data plan.

ml-critic R1: 3 FATAL (F1 sev9 circular synthetic - null guaranteed by DGP; F2 sev8 strawman tab baseline; F3 sev7 standardization/PLE-edge contamination by fraud last step), 3 material (F4 convergence, F5 length-dependence, F6 iid), 1 minor, 1 NIT suppressed. Resolution: harder synthetic control + real data + aggregate baseline + causal-only stats.
Stage A.2: ml-defender R1 against 7 non-suppressed findings

ml-defender R1: empirical_test_agreed. F1-F5 DEFER (9/8/7/6/5) to Step-6 tests (harder control, aggregate baseline, causal-only stats, convergence, length sweep); F6/F7 rebutted. No concessions; sub-claim (a) honestly conceded as unresolvable from PoC.
Stage B round 1: ml-critic-r2 challenges defender R1
ml-critic-r2 round 1: 6 ACCEPT, 1 PARTIAL (F3 7->6, PLE-edge contamination still material until causal-only refit). F1-F2 held at 9/8.
Stage B.2 round 1: ml-defender R2 responds to F3 PARTIAL; is_final_round=false
Round 1 derive_verdict: empirical_test_agreed (F1-F5 ETA via DEFER; F6/F7 defense_wins).
Round 1 not converged (round 1 < min_rounds 2); proceed to round 2
ml-critic-r2 round 2: all 7 ACCEPT

Converged after 2 rounds (B.1 early-exit). empirical_test_agreed. Step-6 requirements: F1 harder synthetic control + real data; F2 tab_aggregate baseline; F3 causal-only standardization/PLE edges; F4 train-to-convergence; F5 length sweep.

User approved full: 6 arms (tab_last, tab_aggregate, seq_raw, seq_ple, seq_dev, seq_raw_shuffle), length axis L{8,32}, >=3 seeds, gated Metaflow flow on real account sequences. Next: intent-watch -> build -> 4 gates -> run.
intent-watch clean after fixing 2 HIGH conflicts (README data source -> demo dataset; plan keeps *Count). Pre-registration LOCKED.
Building gated torch-GRU Metaflow flow for real account sequences (6 arms, L axis, causal-only preprocessing, bootstrap CIs, shuffle lever). determinism=single_worker (torch CPU).

Fixed make_data: targets from split files (7% fraud), history from transactions.parq (was sampling targets from full log -> 1.6% diluted, suspicious). Re-smoke L=8: sane (tab_last 0.145, seq_raw 0.131); EARLY SIGNAL: GRU arms underperform tabular, shuffle doesn't hurt -> weak cross-time amount signal. Lint exit 0.
Gate layer 3: pipeline-reviewer on corrected seq_flow.py
Gate layer 3: pipeline-reviewer PASS on all 5 fidelity invariants (split, reshuffle, axes, metric, sweep/early-stop). Non-blocking.
Gate layer 4 (prove): launched 2x full single_worker runs + determinism-check in background (n_targets 6000/2000/2500, seeds=3, L{8,32}, 6 arms). Awaiting completion.
Gate 4 (prove): single_worker determinism PASS (two max-workers-1 runs identical). All 4 gates green; promotion complete.
Step 6 done via promoted GRU flow (account sequences, L{8,32}, 3 seeds, 6 arms, test-set bootstrap CIs).

Real sequences: T-F2 precondition FAILS (seq_raw-tab_aggregate -0.035*@L8, -0.049*@L32 -> GRU UNDERPERFORMS logreg aggregate). Sub-claim(a) deviation REFUTED (seq_dev-seq_raw -0.023*@L32). Sub-claim(b) PLE no lift (CIs overlap 0). Lever: shuffle doesn't hurt (seq_raw-shuffle ~0) -> no cross-time signal used. Through-line: PLE-on-amount no lift across all 3 cycles.
Metaflow GRU promotion complete: 4/4 gates green (prevent/lint/review/prove single_worker).

Step 7: CONCLUSIONS.md + 2 figures. Sequence model UNDERPERFORMS tabular logreg; deviation & PLE add nothing; GRU ignores history order. Through-line: PLE-on-amount no lift across all 3 cycles. Honest caveat: GRU under-powered (capacity-sensitive).

Step 9: REPORT_ADDENDUM. Recommendation: keep amount raw (log) per step; PLE/deviation add nothing; tabular-beats-GRU is capacity-sensitive (retest at scale). Cross-cycle synthesis: PLE-on-amount never helped real fraud.

Cycle 3 complete (conclusions_only, debate, gated GRU flow 4/4). REFUTED: sequence model doesn't beat tabular for amount; deviation/PLE no lift; history order unused. Through-line across cycles 1-3: PLE-on-transaction-amount never improves real fraud detection.
